summary: Bump npm package to 0.1.6 to publish parent-aware branch guard + todo pr command
description:
Two user-facing changes are on main but not yet on npm:
- Parent-aware branch guard (commit 0baf2a2): closes parents without
  --force when all children are terminal.
- New 'todo pr' command (commit b68e9f2): push + gh pr create with
  ticket-derived title/body, replaces local merge as the default close
  flow.

Until 0.1.6 is published, agents in other repos hit the OLD global
0.1.5 — they still --force parent closes and have no 'todo pr'.

Steps (user-driven; agent cannot publish):
1. Bump version in package.json from 0.1.5 → 0.1.6
2. Commit ('chore: bump to 0.1.6')
3. Tag v0.1.6 and push tag (the release workflow handles the npm publish)
4. After publish, run 'npm i -g @4-r-c-4-n-4/todo@latest' so the local
   global picks up the new build

Done contract: package.json shows 0.1.6; v0.1.6 release exists on
github.com/4-R-C-4-N-4/todo/releases; 'todo --version' on the global
install prints 0.1.6 and 'todo --help' shows the 'pr' command.

resolution.note: Version bumped to 0.1.6 in package.json and published to npm by the user (commit df1d154). Global agents picking up @4-r-c-4-n-4/todo@latest now get the parent-aware branch guard and the 'todo pr' command. Done out-of-band on main rather than a todo/<id> branch since this is a release chore the user drives.
